headline: Jim Cramer Calls RTX A 'Monster' Right Here, Aurora A 'Worthy' Spec
summary: On CNBC's “Mad Money Lightning Round,” Jim Cramer recommended buying RTX Corp, saying it is a “monster” right here. “It's down a lot. It makes no sense,” he added. “It's because there's not enough aircraft servicing, because people feel that people aren't going to fly anymore. Wrong!” RTX, on April 30, raised its quarterly dividend from 68 cents to 73 cents per share. Don't Miss: Think Your ‘Safe' Stocks Protect You? You're Ignoring the Real Growth Triggers — Here's What to Add Now Caught With N

headline: Defense Stocks Are Stabilizing. When to Buy.
summary: The iShares U.S. Aerospace & Defense ETF rallied 23% to $250, then fell 16% to $210. It is now stabilizing around $217.

headline: Stock Market Today: Oil Jumps 5%, S&P 500 Drops As Iran Strikes UAE Port
summary: U.S. stocks sank Monday as an Iranian drone attack on a UAE petroleum site sent Brent above $114, gold tumbled 2%, and FedEx, UPS and GXO cratered after Amazon expanded its logistics business

headline: Supreme Court Refuses To Extend ‘Battlefield Preemption’ Defense To Conduct The Government Never Authorized

headline: RTX Annual Meeting: Calio Touts Record $271B Backlog, Output Gains, and Big 2026 Investment Plans
summary: RTX (NYSE:RTX) held its 2026 annual meeting of shareowners virtually, with Chairman Chris Calio presiding alongside Executive Vice President and General Counsel Raja Maharajh and Senior Vice President and Corporate Secretary Ted Perreault. Management participants included Chief Financial Officer Nei
